skill.name: salesforce-automation
skill.description: Automate Salesforce CRM operations through Composio's Salesforce toolkit via Rube MCP for managing leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, tasks, and SOQL queries.
skill.summary: Execute Salesforce CRM tasks including lead management, contact operations, opportunity tracking, custom SOQL queries, and task workflows through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation-only file (SKILL.md) describing Salesforce CRM operations via Rube MCP. All 67 static analyzer findings are false positives: the detected 'external_commands' are markdown backticks around tool names (e.g., SALESFORCE_SEARCH_LEADS), not shell execution. The 'network' finding references the MCP server URL in documentation. No code execution, shell commands, or network requests exist in this skill.

content.value_statement: Salesforce admins and sales teams need efficient CRM automation without manual clicks. This skill enables Claude and Codex to execute leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, and SOQL queries through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Create, search, update, and list Salesforce leads with assignment rules
Manage contacts and accounts including association between them
Track opportunities through sales pipeline stages
Execute custom SOQL queries with pagination support
Create, update, and complete Salesforce tasks
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection to Salesforce via Composio
Cannot bypass Salesforce OAuth authentication or permission limits
SOQL queries must follow Salesforce syntax and field API naming conventions
Cannot access objects or fields outside your Salesforce org permissions

use_cases.title: Sales Operations Manager
use_cases.description: Automate daily lead intake by creating leads from email inquiries, assigning them to the correct campaigns, and applying lead assignment rules without manual data entry.

use_cases.title: Account Executive
use_cases.description: Track opportunity pipeline by querying deals at specific stages, updating close dates and amounts, and associating contacts with their respective accounts.

use_cases.title: Salesforce Administrator
use_cases.description: Run bulk SOQL queries to audit data quality, update task statuses across multiple records, and retrieve custom object schemas for documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Create and Assign a Lead
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new lead with LastName 'Johnson', Company 'Acme Corp', Email 'johnson@acme.com', and Phone '555-0123'. Then add this lead to the 'Q1 Campaign' and apply lead assignment rules.

prompt_templates.title: Search Contacts and Associate with Account
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for contacts with LastName 'Smith'. For each result, check if they are associated with an account. If not, search for the account named 'Tech Solutions' and associate the contact to that account.

prompt_templates.title: Query and Update Opportunities
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a SOQL query to find all opportunities in the 'Proposal' stage with CloseDate in the next 30 days. For each opportunity, update the StageName to 'Negotiation' and add a note to the description.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Overdue Tasks and Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for all tasks with Status 'Not Started' and ActivityDate before today. Mark each task as complete with Status 'Completed'. Then query all completed tasks and provide a summary count by subject.

output_examples.input: Search for contacts with email containing 'gmail.com'
output_examples.output: Found 3 contacts matching criteria: John Smith (john@gmail.com), Sarah Johnson (sarah.j@gmail.com), Mike Brown (mbrown@gmail.com). Each contact shows LastName, FirstName, Email, Phone, and associated Account Name.

output_examples.input: Create a new opportunity named 'Enterprise Deal' for account 'BigCorp Inc' with StageName 'Prospecting', CloseDate 2026-06-30, and Amount 250000
output_examples.output: Successfully created opportunity: Id='0063h00000ABC123', Name='Enterprise Deal', AccountId='0013h00000DEF456', StageName='Prospecting', CloseDate='2026-06-30', Amount=250000, CreatedDate='2026-02-24T23:54:22Z'

output_examples.input: Run SOQL: SELECT Id, Name, Industry FROM Account WHERE Industry = 'Technology' LIMIT 10
output_examples.output: Query returned 10 accounts in Technology sector. Results include Account Id, Name, and Industry fields. Response indicates done=false with nextRecordsUrl for pagination if more results exist.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Verify Rube MCP connection status shows ACTIVE before running any Salesforce operations
Use SALESFORCE_RUN_SOQL_QUERY to validate data before bulk updates to prevent unintended changes
anti_patterns: Creating leads without checking for duplicates using SALESFORCE_SEARCH_LEADS first
Assuming field display labels match API names (use API names like Account.Name, not 'Account Name')
Ignoring pagination tokens in SOQL query results when processing large datasets

faq.question: How do I set up the Rube MCP connection to Salesforce?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your Claude or Codex configuration. Call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'salesforce'. If the connection is not ACTIVE, follow the returned OAuth link to authorize. Confirm ACTIVE status before running workflows.

faq.question: What is the difference between SALESFORCE_RUN_SOQL_QUERY and SALESFORCE_QUERY?
faq.answer: Both execute SOQL queries. SALESFORCE_QUERY additionally supports pagination using nextRecordsUrl for large result sets. Use SALESFORCE_QUERY when you expect results exceeding standard page limits.

faq.question: Why do my SOQL queries fail with 'unknown field' errors?
faq.answer: SOQL uses Salesforce field API names, not display labels. For example, use Account.Name instead of 'Account Name'. Custom fields end with __c suffix. Use SALESFORCE_GET_ALL_CUSTOM_OBJECTS to discover correct API names.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to delete Salesforce records?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on create, read, and update operations. Deletion capabilities depend on available tools in the Composio Salesforce toolkit. Check SALESFORCE_RUN_SOQL_QUERY documentation for available operations.

faq.question: How do I handle Salesforce ID format differences?
faq.answer: Salesforce IDs are 15 characters (case-sensitive) or 18 characters (case-insensitive). Both formats work for most operations. Use 18-character IDs when performing case-insensitive matching or external integrations.

faq.question: What permissions do I need in Salesforce to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need appropriate object and field permissions in your Salesforce profile or permission sets. The skill operates within your user's existing permissions. Contact your Salesforce admin if you encounter access errors.
